description_long: This object is a retro-style control cabinet, predominantly red with a robust, angular metal body. The front panel features two main sections, each equipped with multiple square and rectangular buttons, toggle switches, and indicator lights, with one section displaying a prominent orange screen. The control surface is slightly inclined for ergonomic access, and the lower portion is solid, providing stability and housing internal components. The top of the cabinet has two sets of horizontal ventilation grilles, likely for heat dissipation. The sides and back are plain, with the back being a solid red panel, and the base is finished with a metallic strip. This type of cabinet is typically found in power management or industrial control rooms within urban infrastructure.
description: A robust, red, retro-style control cabinet with dual control panels, buttons, switches, and a prominent orange display, featuring ventilation grilles on top and a solid base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cabinet's angular profile, red finish, and the overhanging control panel surface.
description_view_1: Front view displaying two control panels with buttons, switches, indicator lights, and an orange screen, as well as the inclined control surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, highlighting the cabinet's depth and the edge of the control panel.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain, solid red panel with a metallic base strip and no visible controls or features.
